prerequisites: A developer identity on the target Dev Portal (signup/approval is per-portal, configured by the portal owner)

verification.name: Portal registration approval
verification.description: Application registration against a published API may require portal-admin approval before credentials appear, depending on portal settings.

mechanisms.description: The door that mints credentials: the Dev Portal's own API. A developer logs in for a portalaccesstoken, creates an application, registers it against a published API, and receives an api_key (Key-Auth) or client_id/client_secret (OIDC/DCR strategy).
The admin door: platform API with a kpat_ personal access token. Can list and approve developer applications but cannot mint a developer's secret — applications belong to developers.

methods.bootstrap: Log in to the Dev Portal as a developer and capture the portalaccesstoken, or provide developer email/password for a basic login step.
Generate a Personal Access Token in the Konnect console (admin plane; cannot mint developer secrets).

credentials.rotation: OIDC/DCR secrets are minted in the wired identity provider (Auth0, Okta, Azure AD) and may be shown once.

scopes.notes: Credential shape depends on the auth strategy the API publisher chose (Key-Auth vs OIDC/DCR); auth_strategy_id must be passed when a portal exposes more than one.

args.name: auth_strategy
args.description: auth_strategy_id when the portal exposes more than one

steps.description: Basic developer login; skipped when KONNECT_PORTAL_TOKEN is already set.

409.message: Application already exists; reading it back.

400.message: No separate credential resource; using app-level credentials.

404.message: No separate credential resource; using app-level credentials.

gaps: The bootstrap: the first portal identity (or kpat_) is still minted by a human in a console — no public, anonymous browser-OAuth flow at a fixed URL.
The developer-facing portal API is versioned per portal and thinly documented publicly; paths and credential field names should be verified per portal.
